Title: The Multi-INstrument Burst ARchive (MINBAR)
Authors: Galloway D.K., in 't Zand J.J.M., Chenevez J., W\"orpel H;, Keek L., Ootes L., Watts A.L., Gisler L., Sanchez-Fernandez C., Kuulkers K.
Table: MINBAR table of thermonuclear bursts
================================================================================
Byte-by-byte Description of file: minbar.txt
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Bytes Format Units Label  Explanations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1- 23 A23    ---         name      Likely burst origin
  25- 27 A3     ---         instr     Instrument label (1)
  29- 43 A15    ---         obsid     Observation ID
  45- 55 F11.5  d           time      Burst start time (MJD UT)
  57- 60 I4     ---         entry     MINBAR burst ID
  62- 67 I6     ---         entry_obs MINBAR observation ID in which this burst falls
  69- 72 I4     ---         bnum      Order of the event within the observation
  74- 77 I4     ---         xref      ? Burst ID in external catalog (G08 or C17)
  79- 79 I1     ---         mult      Number of MINBAR instruments which detected this event [1,2]
  81- 87 F7.2   arcmin      angle     Angle between the source position and pointing axis
  89- 93 F5.3   ---         vigcorr   Vignetting correction factor
  95-105 A11    ---         sflag     Data quality/analysis flags
 107-111 F5.2   ---         rexp      Photospheric radius-expansion flag
 113-117 F5.2   s           rise      Burst rise time
 119-123 F5.1   s           tau       ?=0.0 Ratio of fluence to peak flux, tau
 125-129 F5.1   s         e_tau       ?=0.0 Uncertainty on tau
 131-136 F6.1   s           dur       ?=0.0 Burst duration
 138-143 F6.1   s         e_dur       ?=0.0 Uncertainty on burst duration
 145-150 F6.1   s           edt       ?=0.0 Decay constant for exponential fit to profile
 152-158 F7.3   s         e_edt       ?=0.0 Error on decay constant
 160-167 F8.1   h           tdel      ?=0.0 Time since previous burst from this source
 169-175 F7.1   h           trec      ?=0.0 Inferred recurrence time
 177-182 F6.3   10-9mW/m2   perflx    ?=0.0 Persistent 3-25 keV flux prior to the burst, F_per
 184-189 F6.3   10-9mW/m2 e_perflx    ?=0.0 Uncertainty on persistent flux
 191-196 F6.1   ---         alpha     ?=0.0 Ratio of integrated persistent flux to burst fluence, alpha
 198-203 F6.1   ---       e_alpha     ?=0.0 Uncertainty on alpha
 205-209 F5.3   ---         bc        ?=0.0 Bolometric correction adopted for persistent flux
 211-215 F5.3   ---       e_bc        ?=0.0 Uncertainty on bolometric correction
 217-222 F6.4   ---         gamma     ?=0.0 Ratio of persistent flux to peak PRE burst flux, gamma
 224-229 F6.3   ---         sc        ?=0.0 Soft colour
 231-236 F6.3   ---         hc        ?=0.0 Hard colour
 238-243 F6.3   ---         s_z       ?=0.0 Position on the colour-colour diagram, S_Z
 245-250 F6.2   ct/cm2/s    pflux     ?=0.0 Peak photon flux
 252-256 F5.2   ct/cm2/s  e_pflux     ?=0.0 Uncertainty on peak photon flux
 258-265 F8.3   ct/cm2      fluen     ?=0.0 Integrated photon flux
 267-273 F7.3   ct/cm2    e_fluen     ?=0.0 Uncertainty on integrated photon flux
 275-280 F6.2   10-9mW/m2   bpflux    ?=0.0 Bolometric peak flux (from blackbody fit)
 282-286 F5.2   10-9mW/m2 e_bpflux    ?=0.0 Uncertainty on bolometric peak flux
 288-291 F4.2   keV         kT        ?=0.0 Blackbody temperature kT at burst peak
 293-296 F4.2   keV       e_kT        ?=0.0 Uncertainty on blackbody temperature kT at burst peak
 298-303 F6.1   ---         rad       ?=0.0 Blackbody normalisation at burst peak (2)
 305-309 F5.1   ---       e_rad       ?=0.0 Uncertainty on blackbody normalisation at burst peak (2)
 311-316 F6.4   10-6mJ/m2   bfluen    ?=0.0 Bolometric fluence (integrated bolometric flux), E_b
 318-323 F6.4   10-6mJ/m2 e_bfluen    ?=0.0 Uncertainty on bolometric fluence
 325-344 A20    ---         refs      References for this burst
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note (1): XPn for RXTE/PCA bursts, where n specifies the number of PCUs on (see
          the paper); SWc for BeppoSAX/WFC bursts, where c gives the camera
          number; IJu for INTEGRAL/JEM-X bursts, with u the unit number, or X
          where both units were active
     (2): Units of (R/d), where R is in km and distance d in units of 10 kpc,
          as for the bbodyrad model in XSpec; Dorman & Arnaud (2001)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
